title: DNS resolution over TCP broken in custom bridge network
body:
### Issue Description

Whenever a DNS request has to fall back to TCP, the podman (aardvark?) DNS fails to resolve when using a custom network.

UDP is unaffected in all cases, using the default network is unaffected in TCP and UDP. But as soon as truncation forces fallback to TCP when using a custom network, this error occurs:
```$ nslookup -q=TXT s1._domainkey.domain.tld
;; Truncated, retrying in TCP mode.
;; Connection to 10.89.1.1#53(10.89.1.1) for s1[...] failed: connection refused.
```

### Steps to reproduce the issue

1. `podman network create test` 
2. `podman run -it --network test docker.io/mariadb /bin/bash`
3. `apt update&&apt install -y dnsutils`
4. `nslookup -q=TXT s1._domainkey.cyber-biene.de`

the request here is a placeholder, most DNS requests fit within the UDP packet size and don't trigger the issue

### Describe the results you received

```
;; Truncated, retrying in TCP mode.
;; Connection to 10.89.1.1#53(10.89.1.1) for s1._domainkey.cyber-biene.de failed: connection refused.
;; no servers could be reached
```

### Describe the results you expected

DNS resolution works as it does without a custom network

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

No

### Additional environment details

Wether the DHCP provided DNS or 8.8.8.8/8.8.4.4 is used on the system makes no difference.

aardvark and netavark are installed, this issue is also reproducable on a new podman install.

```
$ podman network inspect testnet  
[
     {
          "name": "test",
          "id": "411a1060feba9796b6c2b1759fdd325eb41aef997c131fd23369e908743461da",
          "driver": "bridge",
          "network_interface": "podman2",
          "created": "2024-08-28T20:27:55.30842391+02:00",
          "subnets": [
               {
                    "subnet": "10.89.1.0/24",
                    "gateway": "10.89.1.1"
               }
          ],
          "ipv6_enabled": false,
          "internal": false,
          "dns_enabled": true,
          "network_dns_servers": [
               "212.227.123.17"
          ],
          "ipam_options": {
               "driver": "host-local"
          }
     }
]
```

### Additional information

Issue happens on two different AlmaLinux systems, one Aarch64, this one is amd64. The Aarch64 one is essentially a clean linux install and in a completely different environment. Both are VPSes on different providers.

comments.body: TCP support was added in aardvark-dns 1.12, https://github.com/containers/aardvark-dns/releases/tag/v1.12.0
Thanks for the tip, I just had the same problem. Everything works now
I can also report that it is fixed, thank you!
